response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced senior engineer with strong software craftsmanship, testing culture, and some genuine low-latency distributed-systems work (millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic). However, that work was in DLang, not C++ — his documented C++ experience is roughly one year (National Instruments plus thesis work), far short of the 8-year minimum. His recent career is web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js, React), and he has no formal technical-leadership or cloud-platform-architecture role, making him a weak fit for a C++-centric compute-platform tech lead position.
strengths: Real high-scale, low-latency systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) at ad-tech scale
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, plus mentoring via workshops and courses
Broad polyglot background with infrastructure exposure (Kubernetes, Docker, AWS CDK, DevOps setup)
concerns: C++ experience (~1 year) falls far below the 8-year minimum qualification
No formal technical-leadership title or documented experience leading teams/projects across organizations
Recent focus is product-oriented web development, not cloud compute platforms or large-scale infrastructure
